message:
fix(core): cursor EOF safety, monotonic time-travel timestamps, and LIKE prefix coverage

Three logically distinct improvements to connection.rs:

1. Virtual table cursor EOF safety (same pattern as extension crates):
   - HtmMetricsCursor, SchemaAwareTestCursor, TxnAwareTestCursor all
     return NULL/0 when column()/rowid() called past EOF
   - FsqliteCachePagesCursor guards column() on eof()
   - Added tests: test_htm_metrics_cursor_past_end_returns_null_and_zero_rowid,
     test_schema_aware_test_cursor_past_end_returns_null_and_zero_rowid,
     test_txn_aware_test_cursor_past_end_returns_null

2. Time-travel snapshot improvements:
   - Snapshot timestamps are now guaranteed monotonic: each new snapshot
     gets max(wall_clock, previous_timestamp + 1), preventing two rapid
     commits from sharing a timestamp (which broke timestamp-based
     time-travel lookups)
   - Refactored snapshot resolution into resolve_time_travel_snapshot_entry()
     which returns the full entry (commit_seq + timestamp + db) instead of
     just the db clone — enables richer diagnostics
   - Timestamp-based time-travel queries now work correctly: test renamed
     from test_time_travel_select_timestamp_returns_error to
     test_time_travel_select_timestamp_returns_historical_state
   - Added test: time_travel_snapshot_capture_keeps_timestamps_monotonic

3. Planner LIKE prefix range scan directive:
   - planner_directive_covering_access_kind now recognizes LIKE prefix
     range scans for covering index selection
   - Added test: test_planner_select_directive_covering_like_prefix_range

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
